data.title: Dollar Set For Weekly Drop Ahead Of US-Iran Peace Talks
data.description:
The dollar on Friday was heading for its largest weekly drop since January as other currencies gained on optimism that a ceasefire in the Gulf will hold and oil shipping will resume.

Further direction for markets is likely to ​hang on the outcome of weekend talks between the U.S. and Iran in Islamabad.

The dollar had towered in ‌March as one of the few bastions of safety as the U.S. and Israeli war on Iran sent oil prices rocketing, hit stocks and gold and inflation worries sank bonds.

But since a shaky ceasefire was agreed on Tuesday those positions are being unwound, with the U.S. dollar index losing 1.3% ​so far this week.

The euro has rallied through its 200-day moving average this week to trade at $1.1690, a break ​of chart resistance that opens the way to further gains.

The risk-sensitive Australian and New Zealand dollars are ⁠looking at weekly rises of nearly 3% on the dollar, with the Aussie trading just above 70 cents and the kiwi ​at $0.5847. Sterling has shot up 1.8% this week and above its 200-day moving average to $1.3424.

Even the yen , under intense pressure from Japan’s ​low interest rates, government spending plans and the country’s dependence on imported oil, is just above recent lows at 159.2 to the dollar.

“People were buying the U.S. dollar when the war was at its most intense moment and now they’re selling as the tail risk of a really bad outcome ​has faded quite a bit,” said Jason Wong, senior strategist at BNZ in Wellington.

“Even though it still looks a bit shaky, ​the ceasefire removing that tail risk is important from a sentiment point of view,” he said, though noting that could turn around very quickly ‌if anticipated ⁠weekend peace talks don’t yield progress.

In the first 24 hours of the ceasefire, just a single oil products tanker and five dry bulk carriers sailed through the Strait of Hormuz, which before the war accommodated about 140 ships a day and about a fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas flows.

Iranian officials arrived in Islamabad on Thursday and a U.S. delegation, led by Vice ​President JD Vance, arrives on Friday ​to discuss what investors ⁠hope can be a lasting peace.

“If there’s positive talks, that would be dollar negative. And if we get to Monday and talks went badly and there’s still a lack of ships…things could turn ​around quickly,” said Wong.

South Korea’s central bank kept its policy interest rate steady on Friday, as expected, ​leaving the won ⁠at 1,478 to the dollar, having recovered from beyond 1,500.

The week’s dollar softness has sent China’s yuan – which has never really fallen since war began at the end of February – to its strongest levels since 2023. In offshore trade it sat at 6.83 per dollar on Friday.

“The ⁠CNY has ​been a surprising winner of the Iran war, despite China’s role as the ​largest oil importer in the world,” said ING economist Lynn Song.

“At least a few market participants have mentioned re-evaluating the ‘China risk premium’ amid rising global uncertainty elsewhere, ​which has led to China looking more and more like the adult in the room.”

Reuters

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Close Higher As Gains Return
data.description:
Hong Kong equities rebounded on April 10, snapping a cautious spell as investor sentiment improved modestly despite lingering global uncertainties.

The Hang Seng Index rose 0.55% to 25,893.54, gaining 141.14 points, with buying interest returning to select large-cap names and lifting the broader market.

The advance comes even as investors remain wary of external headwinds, including geopolitical tensions and energy-driven inflation risks. Still, the day’s gains suggest pockets of resilience, supported by bargain hunting and selective inflows.

Bottom line: Hong Kong stocks edged higher, but the recovery remains tentative as global risks continue to cap stronger upside momentum.

data.title: Singapore Stocks End Flat As Caution Caps Gains
data.description:
Singapore equities closed largely unchanged on April 10, with investors turning cautious amid lingering geopolitical tensions and mixed regional cues.

The Straits Times Index (STI) finished broadly flat, reflecting a pause in momentum after recent volatility across global markets. The muted close mirrors a “cautious calm” seen across Asia, as traders weighed external risks including Middle East developments and energy price swings.

Market sentiment remained fragile, with gains in select sectors offset by weakness elsewhere, keeping the benchmark range-bound. The broader tone suggests investors are reluctant to take aggressive positions heading into fresh global catalysts.

Despite the subdued session, the STI has shown resilience in recent months, supported by steady economic fundamentals and institutional flows, even as external shocks from oil price spikes to geopolitical tension-continue to cloud the outlook.

Bottom line: Singapore stocks are treading water, with investors staying defensive as global uncertainty continues to dictate near-term direction.

data.title: HSIF Faces Resistance At 26,000 Pts, Downside Bias Intact
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd maintained its bearish stance on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), advising traders to hold short positions as the index remains capped below key resistance levels.

The research house said HSIF rose 204 points to close at 25,923 points, although the technical setup continues to favour the downside. The index traded between 26,094 points and 25,645 points during the session, before edging higher in evening trade to around 25,964 points.

Despite forming a bullish candlestick, RHB noted that gains were rejected near immediate resistance, resulting in a long upper shadow and failure to form a higher high. The index also remained below its 50-day simple moving average, indicating that bearish momentum still dominates.

RHB expects the ongoing rebound to be limited, with resistance seen at 26,000 points, while maintaining that the index could resume its downward trend in the near term.

The research house advised maintaining short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage risk.

On the downside, initial support is identified at 24,500 points, followed by a lower support level at 23,500 points, reinforcing the negative outlook for HSIF in the coming sessions.

data.title: Market Snapshot: Crypto Holds Key Levels But Lacks Momentum In Choppy Week
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market saw heightened volatility in the week of April 6 to 10, with Bitcoin and Ethereum trading in choppy ranges amid global macro uncertainty and US-China trade tensions.

Bitcoin fluctuated around key psychological levels, briefly dipping mid-week before staging a modest rebound as bargain-hunting emerged. However, gains were capped as investors remained cautious amid broader Wall Street jitters.

Ethereum followed a similar pattern, fluctuating within a tight band and failing to sustain momentum, while broader altcoin markets remained highly selective with mixed performance across sectors.

Market direction was largely shaped by external macro factors, including evolving expectations around interest rates and global economic growth, with sentiment also influenced by policy signals from the Federal Reserve.

Despite the lack of strong upside momentum, major cryptocurrencies held key support levels, reflecting a degree of underlying resilience even as conviction remained limited.

Overall, the week highlighted a consolidation phase for crypto markets, with investors staying defensive and selective while awaiting clearer macro catalysts to drive the next decisive move.

data.title: Stock Picks: UWC And Unisem
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd has identified UWC Bhd and Unisem (M) Bhd as potential trading opportunities, citing bullish technical breakouts supported by strong momentum.

The research house said UWC has broken above its RM4.35 resistance level on high volume, forming a bullish candlestick that signals strengthening upward momentum. Based on this, the stock is expected to test the RM4.60 level, followed by RM4.80 if momentum sustains. However, a decline below the RM4.15 support level would invalidate the bullish outlook.

For Unisem, RHB noted that the counter has also staged a bullish breakout after closing above the RM2.85 resistance level. The positive price action indicates renewed buying interest, with the stock potentially advancing towards RM3.05 and subsequently RM3.25.

The research house cautioned that the bullish setup for Unisem would be negated if the share price falls below the RM2.65 support level.

RHB said both counters are exhibiting strong technical signals, suggesting continued upside potential in the near term, barring a breakdown below key support levels.

data.title: HSIF Still In Bearish Mode, RHB
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its bearish outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) following fresh negative price action.

The index fell 229 points to close at 25,719 points on Thursday after trading as low as 25,658 points during the session. It later rebounded in the evening session, gaining 181 points to last trade at 25,900 points.

RHB Research said the latest decline reinforces the view that resistance levels remain firm in the current bearish environment, while support levels are weak. The research house expects the index to trend lower once the current sideways movement completes, as bearish momentum continues to build.

Immediate resistance levels are seen at 26,000 points and 26,600 points, while support levels are pegged at 24,500 points and 23,500 points.

The research house advised traders to maintain short positions initiated at 26,367 points, with a stop-loss set at 26,600 points to manage downside risks.

data.title: STI Opens Higher As Markets Cautious Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Singapore equities opened marginally higher, tracking cautious regional sentiment as concerns over the fragile Middle East ceasefire weighed on global markets.

The Straits Times Index (STI) rose 8.31 points, or 0.17%, to 4,985.39 as at 9.15am, with 146 gainers outpacing 83 decliners. Trading volume stood at 186.57 million shares valued at S$241.42 million.

Blue-chip stocks were mixed in early trade. DBS edged up 0.23% to S$57.33, while OCBC Bank rose to S$22.39 and UOB traded at S$37.30. Singtel stood at S$4.94, while Singapore Airlines was at S$6.62.

The cautious tone follows renewed concerns over the durability of the Middle East ceasefire, with Iran reportedly closing the Strait of Hormuz and oil prices rebounding sharply. U.S. crude futures rose toward US$100 a barrel, raising fresh inflation concerns.

data.title: Weekly Snapshot: Kospi Slips In Choppy Week Amid Tariff Fears And Tech Weakness
data.description:
South Korean stocks ended the April 6–10 trading week on a cautious note, with the KOSPI edging lower as investors grappled with persistent US-China trade tensions and uneven global risk sentiment.

The benchmark index traded in a choppy and directionless range throughout the week, with early stability fading into mild losses as profit-taking emerged and tariff-related concerns dampened risk appetite.

Technology heavyweights led the declines, reflecting ongoing sensitivity to global semiconductor demand uncertainty, while export-oriented sectors such as autos also saw intermittent pressure. Financials and select defensive names offered limited support but were not enough to offset broader weakness.

Despite pockets of resilience, sentiment remained fragile as investors continued to monitor global equity trends, particularly movements on Wall Street and shifting expectations around trade policy direction.

Overall, the week reflected a market in consolidation mode, with investors staying selective and defensive as they await clearer signals on global trade dynamics and the broader growth outlook.

data.title: Tech, Property And Construction Stocks Power Bursa To Higher Close
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the day broadly higher on April 10, lifted by strong buying momentum in technology, property and construction counters.

At the close, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 5.07 points, or 0.3%, to 1,691.31 from previous finish of 1,686.24, after trading between 1,685.66 and 1,692.97 during the session.

Gains were mirrored across the broader market, with the FBM 70 climbing 122.62 points (0.71%) to 17,320.97, while the FBM Emas added 53.78 points (0.44%) to 12,412.66. The F4GBM advanced 4.19 points (0.42%) to 1,001.87 and the FBM Shariah edged up 31.19 points (0.25%) to 12,289.33.

Market activity was robust, with total turnover reaching 2.89 billion shares worth RM2.61 billion, reflecting sustained investor participation.

Trading interest remained concentrated in lower-priced and thematic counters. Borneo Oil Bhd dominated volumes, followed by Top Glove Corporation Bhd, Zetrix AI Bhd, Sunway Healthcare Holdings Bhd and Tanco Holdings Bhd. Top Glove was among the notable actives to edge higher, gaining 1.5 sen to 80.5 sen, while Zetrix AI also posted a modest uptick.

Overall, the market’s positive close underscores improving sentiment, with sector-specific buying in growth-oriented counters helping to offset mild profit-taking elsewhere.

data.title: Tesla Stock Drops For Eighth Week In A Row
data.description:
Tesla stock dropped for the eighth week in a row as its rough 2026 start rolls on and first quarter earnings come in later this month.

Tesla stock, though slightly higher in early Friday trade, is down 3.2% for the week, notching its eighth straight week of losses. The stock is down a 22.4% for the year, whereas the S&P 500 is basically flat.

While Tesla does trade seasonally in most years, with the first half usually challenged, bulls will be hard on the lookout for catalysts, seeking anything to get the stock movingThe Elon Musk-led company is slated to reveal version three of its Optimus robot sometime this month. No updates on that have been seen recently, leaving an April update in limbo. Meanwhile, Tesla has not sold a single robot yet, though Musk claims robots are being used in limited capacities in Tesla factories.

Tesla’s Cybercab did hit its production milestone in Q1, with reports showing Cybercabs sitting in parking lots at Giga Austin. When these EVs will be on sale is another story, as robotaxi updates have also been hard to come by.

Tesla is still operating its robotaxi and ride-hailing services in Austin, Texas, and the San Francisco Bay Area, but the rides are still supervised with safety drivers in each vehicle

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher Tracking Wall Street Gains Ahead Of US-Iran Talks
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, tracking Wall Street’s overnight gains as investors remained cautiously optimistic ahead of US-Iran ceasefire talks this weekend.

The benchmark FBM KLCI rose 2.82 points, or 0.17%, to 1,689.06 in early trade.

Broader market indices also advanced. The FBM70 gained 38.69 points, or 0.22%, to 17,237.04, while the FBM Emas Index rose 25.25 points, or 0.20%, to 12,384.13. The FBM Shariah Index added 13.42 points, or 0.11%, to 12,271.56, and the FBM ACE Index edged up 2.11 points, or 0.21%, to 999.79.

Market breadth was positive, with buying interest seen across selected large-cap and mid-cap stocks. Among actives, Bumi Armada rose 0.5 sen to 38 sen, while Jiankun International gained 1 sen to 6.5 sen.

On the broader market, Hong Leong Financial Group led the gainers, rising 26 sen to RM19.02, followed by ATLAN Holdings and Crescendo Corporation. On the downside, Nestle Malaysia fell RM1.14 to RM98.96, while Petronas Dagangan and IHH Healthcare also declined.

The positive tone follows gains on Wall Street, where all three major indices closed higher amid easing concerns over the Middle East conflict. The Dow Jones rose 0.58%, the S&P 500 gained 0.62% and the Nasdaq advanced 0.83%.

data.title: Gold Prices Continue Upward Trajectory, RHB Advises Caution
data.description:
Gold prices continued their upward trajectory on Thursday, with COMEX gold rising US$40.80 to close at US$4,818, as improving momentum fuels expectations of a potential test of the key US$5,000 resistance level.

In a technical note, RHB Investment Bank said the precious metal opened at US$4,744.80, touching an intraday low of US$4,718.60 before climbing to a high of US$4,826 and settling near session highs.

The latest price action, coupled with an upward-trending Relative Strength Index (RSI), signals a reacceleration in bullish momentum, suggesting gold may attempt to break above its 50-day simple moving average (SMA).

A successful move above this level could further improve market sentiment and reinforce the current upward trend.

However, RHB cautioned that strong selling pressure is likely to emerge near the US$5,000 mark, which remains a critical resistance level for the commodity.

Despite the recent rebound, the research house maintained a cautious stance, noting that a break below the 20-day SMA could trigger a renewed correction, potentially pushing prices back towards lower support levels.

As such, RHB retained its negative trading bias until gold achieves a decisive close above US$5,000.

Immediate support for gold is seen at US$4,600, followed by a stronger floor at US$4,400. On the upside, resistance is pegged at US$5,000, with a higher resistance level at US$5,250.

RHB advised traders to maintain existing short positions initiated at US$4,605.70, with a stop-loss set at US$5,000 to manage risk amid ongoing price volatility.

data.title: Banking Stocks Lift Bursa Malaysia At Midday As KLCI Edges Higher
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the morning session on a firmer note, supported by buying interest in banking heavyweights led by Public Bank Bhd and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd.

At 12:30pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 2.49 points, or 0.14%, to 1,688.73 from its close of 1,686.24 on April 9, after moving between an intraday high of 1,692.97 and a low of 1,685.66.

The broader market was also positive, with the FBM 70 climbing 92.72 points (0.54%) to 17,291.07, while the FBM Emas added 33.67 points (0.27%) to 12,392.55. The FBM Shariah edged up 13.82 points (0.11%) to 12,271.96 and the F4GBM gained 2.29 points (0.23%) to 999.97.

Market sentiment remained constructive, with gainers outpacing losers 512 to 377, while 520 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,299 stocks were untraded and 13 suspended. Turnover stood at 1.43 billion shares valued at RM1.09 billion.

Banking stocks underpinned the index’s advance, with Public Bank rose four sen to RM4.60, CIMB added seven sen to RM7.45 and RHB Bank Bhd jumped 14 sen to RM8. Malayan Banking Bhd was unchanged at RM11.16, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd slipped four sen to RM14.12.

Among the top gainers, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd advanced 74 sen to RM29.82, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd gained 42 sen to RM29.76 and Malayan Cement Bhd rose 27 sen to RM6.81. Sunway Construction Group Bhd and Vitrox Corporation Bhd also posted solid gains.

On the downside, Nestle (M) Bhd led decliners, falling RM1.10 to RM99, followed by Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd, which dropped 44 sen to RM21.76. Petronas Dagangan Bhd, Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and MISC Bhd also ended lower.

In terms of actives, Borneo Oil Bhd dominated trading volumes, followed by Zetrix AI Bhd, Sunway Healthcare Holdings Bhd, Top Glove Corporation Bhd and Tanco Holdings BHd, reflecting continued retail participation in lower-priced and thematic counters.

Overall, the market’s mild uptrend at midday reflects selective buying in index-linked banking stocks amid cautious broader participation.

data.title: Market Review: Hang Seng In Consolidation Mode As Trade Tensions Persist
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks closed the April 6-10 trading week on a softer note, with the Hang Seng Index (HSI) edging lower amid persistent US-China trade tensions and cautious investor sentiment.

The market traded unevenly throughout the week, with early gains giving way to profit-taking as tariff fears and Wall Street volatility dampened risk appetite.

Tech and property stocks led declines, reflecting sensitivity to global growth concerns, while financials and select defensives provided limited support.

Turnover remained moderate as investors adopted a defensive stance, closely tracking external cues from Wall Street and mainland economic data.

Overall, the HSI stayed in consolidation mode, with sentiment fragile as markets await a clearer direction on trade policies and their broader economic impact.

data.title: Ringgit Extends Gains Against Dollar On Improved Risk Appetite
data.description:
The ringgit remained firm against the US dollar at the opening on Friday, supported by sustained demand for the local currency amid improving risk appetite among traders and investors as reported by Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency strengthened to 3.9750/9935 against the greenback from Thursday’s close of 3.9795/9845.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) fell 0.32 per cent to 98.819 points. In contrast, the two- and 10-year US Treasury yields fell by two basis points to 3.77 per cent and 4.28 per cent.

He noted that the US gross domestic product for the fourth quarter of 2025 (4Q2025) fell to 0.5 per cent from consensus estimates of 0.7 per cent, down from 4.4 per cent in 3Q2025.

“This suggests the US economy is on a weaker trajectory, and the Core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation, which moderated to three per cent in February from 3.1 per cent previously, suggests the Federal Reserve might cut interest rates at some point this year.

“As such, this could be positive for the ringgit. Nonetheless, guarded sentiment over the ceasefire would keep a lid on the pace of ringgit appreciation,” he told Bernama.

Meanwhile, the ringgit opened mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.

It strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.4981/5099 from 2.5028/5061 at Thursday’s close, but was marginally lower against the euro at 4.6468/6684 from 4.6457/6515 and eased versus the British pound to 5.3372/3621 from 5.3337/3404.

The local currency traded mixed against ASEAN currencies.

It fell against the Singapore dollar to 3.1216/1366 from 3.1204/1246 at yesterday’s close, but appreciated against the Thai baht to 12.3859/4517 from 12.3976/4201.

It was little changed against the Philippine peso at 6.65/6.69 from 6.66/6.67 previously, while nearly flat against the Indonesian rupiah at 232.6/233.7 compared with 232.8/233.2.

data.title: Weekly Snapshot: STI Trades Range-Bound As Caution Persists Amid Global Uncertainty
data.description:
Singapore stocks ended the week of April 6–10 on a steady but cautious note, with the Straits Times Index (STI) moving within a narrow range as investors weighed mixed global cues and interest rate uncertainties.

The market saw choppy, range-bound trading throughout the week, with early stability capped by profit-taking toward the later sessions as sentiment remained guarded. Concerns over global trade developments and macroeconomic signals kept gains modest.

Banking heavyweights, including DBS Group Holdings, Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation and United Overseas Bank, provided underlying support, helping to anchor the index, while technology and consumer counters faced intermittent selling pressure. Commodity-linked stocks, meanwhile, saw selective interest.

Trading activity remained moderate, reflecting a wait-and-see approach as investors assessed global inflation signals and the policy outlook from the Federal Reserve. External factors, including Wall Street performance and regional market trends, continued to guide direction.

Despite pockets of resilience, the overall tone remained cautious and selective, with investors remaining defensive while awaiting clearer signals on global growth and the trajectory of monetary policy.

data.title: F&N, MPI Lead Gainers As Advancers Dominate Bursa
data.description:
Gainers took the lead on Bursa Malaysia, with Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd (F&N) and Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd (MPI) spearheading advances as market breadth turned firmly positive.

Advancers outpaced decliners 663 to 400, while 562 counters were unchanged. A total of 1,083 stocks were untraded and 13 suspended, reflecting selective buying interest across the market.

Among the top gainers, F&N rose 72 sen to RM29.80, while MPI added 46 sen to RM29.80. Sunway Construction Group Bhd climbed 32 sen to RM6.85, UWC Bhd gained 24 sen to RM4.38, and Malayan Cement Bhd advanced 22 sen to RM6.76.

On the flip side, Nestle (M) Bhd led the decliners, sliding RM1.10 to RM99. Petronas Dagangan Bhd fell 60 sen to RM20.80, Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd shed 26 sen to RM21.94, while QL Resources Bhd and Arka Bhd also closed lower.

Overall, the session was marked by stronger buying momentum in selected heavyweights and mid-cap stocks, with gainers clearly outpacing losers despite pockets of profit-taking in defensive names.

data.title: Market Recap: Bursa Edges Higher Amid Trade Jitters And Choppy Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia wrapped up the week of April 6 to 10 on a steady note, with the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) posting modest gains as investors weighed global trade jitters and volatile external cues.

The benchmark index saw choppy movements throughout the week, reflecting mixed sentiment driven by US tariff fears and firmer commodity prices. Early-week advances were tempered by profit-taking and selective positioning toward the end of the week.

Trading activity remained moderate, with investors adopting a wait-and-see approach amid uncertainty over inflation signals and global growth outlook.

Sector-wise, plantation counters drew interest on the back of elevated crude palm oil prices, while consumer and technology stocks faced intermittent selling pressure. Banking heavyweights provided some support, helping to limit broader downside.

Market direction continued to be influenced by external factors, particularly Wall Street performance and commodity market volatility, with Bursa often mirroring overnight global trends.

Overall, the week underscored a resilient tone in the market, as investors remained selective and defensive, awaiting clearer signals on the trajectory of global trade tensions and their impact on Malaysia’s economic outlook.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 10, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 10, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.
